feedback.text: The Digital Fairness Act aims to protect consumers online from problematic practices which should also include the protection of video games. Many video games nowadays are being made unplayable by the publishers without prior notice even though they were paid for by the customers without a specific end-of-service date. This constitutes a form of planned obsolescence which is detrimental to the customers and to the preservation of games as an artform. Publishers should provide a clear way to retain the playability of games even after they are taken out of service. This issue is also tackled by the consumer movement Stop Killing Games while the European Citizens' Initiatives' Stop Destroying Videogames petition has already amassed over 1.4 million votes.
